title: invalid internal status, try resetting the pause process with \"/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/bin/podman system migrate\": setting up the process: open /run/user/3271/libpod/tmp/pause.pid: no such file or directory
body:
New one-off flake, seen in [debian root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5561765895864320/html/sys-podman-debian-12-root-host-boltdb.log.html):
```
not ok 232 podman image scp transfer
...
# podman image scp some9724dude@localhost::foo.bar/nonesuch/c_v59yduh5oc:mytag foobar:123
error creating temporary file: No such file or directory
time="2023-05-02T13:25:28Z"
      level=error
      msg="invalid internal status, try resetting the pause process with \"podman system migrate\": 
         setting up the process: open /run/user/3271/libpod/tmp/pause.pid: no such file or directory"
Error: exit status 1
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Still happening as of yesterday

* debian-12 : sys podman debian-12 root host boltdb
  * PR #18774
    * [06-05 06:29](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6640645104730112/html/sys-podman-debian-12-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00236) in [sys] podman image scp transfer
  * PR #18640
    * [05-24 13:32](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6518562941042688/html/sys-podman-debian-12-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00232) in [sys] podman image scp transfer
  * PR #18385
    * [05-02-2023 09:39](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5561765895864320/html/sys-podman-debian-12-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00232) in [sys] podman image scp transfer
* debian-12 : sys podman debian-12 root host sqlite
  * PR #17831
    * [05-03-2023 20:33](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5718289570070528/html/sys-podman-debian-12-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00232) in [sys] podman image scp transfer

Reproducible on Debian 12 / bookworm as well. `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/libpod` gets recreated on relogin but `tmp` subfolder of it does not. Recreating the tmp directory prior to running podmn solves the issue but the running containers dont survive logout.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Last seen June 23. All instances were on Debian 12, and we're now running 13, so I'm tempted to la-la-la sweep this under the rug.

@basilgello does this remain a problem/priority for you? And, have you tried (as root) `loginctl enable-linger YOURUSERNAME`?
I do that and/or tmuxing but the patch is definitely on my list
No instances of the flake since my last post. All I can do here is close.
